name: Accelerate (HuggingFace Distributed Training)

performance.notes: Training throughput wrapper; adds minimal overhead; enables multi-GPU/node scaling

agent_readiness.notes: HuggingFace library for hardware-agnostic distributed PyTorch training. Single code change enables: single GPU, multi-GPU (DDP), multi-node, TPU, Apple MPS, mixed precision (fp16/bf16), 8-bit quantization. Accelerator class wraps model, optimizer, dataloader. `accelerate launch` for distributed execution. DeepSpeed and FSDP integration for large model training. No change to training loop structure. Used by TRL, diffusers, and other HF libraries internally.

gotchas: Not all operations are safe across devices without careful coding
Debugging distributed training issues requires experience
best_when: Scaling PyTorch training from 1 GPU to multi-GPU/multi-node
Mixed precision training (fp16/bf16) with automatic loss scaling
Large model training with DeepSpeed ZeRO or FSDP
Hardware-agnostic training code (GPU → TPU → MPS without changes)
avoid_when: Single-GPU training where standard PyTorch suffices
summary: Accelerate enables hardware-agnostic distributed PyTorch training with minimal code changes. Single Accelerator wrapper adds multi-GPU, multi-node, TPU, and Apple MPS support. Mixed precision (fp16/bf16), DeepSpeed ZeRO, and FSDP integration. Used internally by HuggingFace's TRL, diffusers, and transformers. Essential for scaling LLM training beyond a single GPU without rewriting training loops.
